value_raw: Cheap and freely available — Goa deliberately keeps excise duty low as state tourism policy
notes: A genuine positive contrast with most of India; closer to Guatemala's/Belize's normal-licensed-availability shape
Numbeo figure

value_raw: Pre-monsoon 30-35°C; monsoon Jun-Sep (heavy, closures); dry season Oct-Feb 28-32°C daytime
notes: Warmth stated as fact, not a demerit, per the project's climate-neutrality convention

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Numbeo sample flagged thin (15 contributors, 107 entries in past 12 months) — see the itemized breakdown rows below instead of a single blended figure
nomads.com aggregate: $733/mo comfortable single, $977/mo family, $1,092/mo higher-spend single traveler

Manipal Hospital Goa (Dona Paula, Panaji), nationally accredited (NABH), 24/7 ED — the real anchor for the whole state, not walkable from the Anjuna-Assagao corridor. Exact drive times from specific villages not confirmed this pass

fact_label: Elevated sexual-assault risk for female travelers
value_raw: Yes
notes: Stated plainly across multiple sources as a distinct, elevated concern relative to many other parts of India — not folded into a generic safety score

value_raw: 3-4 well-run coworking spaces opened in the past 2 years
notes: No hard headcount found — a real gap

value_raw: Public hospitals accessible, pay out-of-pocket, long non-critical waits; private is the real functional door, no residency/citizenship gate
notes: Foreign-patient services genuinely exist (Manipal's own international-patient program, part of a broader Indian medical-tourism industry)

fact_label: Healthcare access continuity, given no long-stay visa
value_raw: Access is private-pay and structurally capped/interrupted by tourist-visa duration limits (30-day non-extendable, or 180 days/year on multi-year e-visas) — never continuous, never public-fallback-backed
notes: Downstream compound effect of the Visa & legal pathway ease criterion's own finding (`India/visa-legal.md#the-headline-difference-from-most-of-this-projects-other-candidates`) — no public/subsidized route exists because no long-term residency status exists to attach one to; the dominant reason the healthcare score doesn't land higher despite genuinely decent, affordable private care

value_raw: A genuinely named problem — outages, inconsistent throughput, even inside coworking spaces
notes: Some cafes reportedly have better, more consistent upload speeds than the corridor's own coworking spaces
₹700-1,200/month (Jio Fiber, BSNL, ACT fiber); no measured (as opposed to advertised) speed-test data found this pass

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Distinct mechanism from the tourist-assault pattern above

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Only forum/aggregator-guide synthesis found, not a direct lived account
Thin Numbeo sample, flagged explicitly by the source file

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: The qualitative assault findings above stand without a quantified index cross-check

value_raw: Most common crime overall
notes: The baseline risk most visitors actually encounter, distinct from the sharper assault/harassment pattern above

fact_label: Police responsiveness to violence against women
value_raw: Often unresponsive, per named sourcing; swift action shown on specific high-profile incidents
notes: An institutional-response gap distinct from the underlying crime rate itself

fact_label: Private hospital capability (Manipal Hospitals Goa, Victor Hospital)
value_raw: Nationally accredited (NABH) multi-specialty (Manipal) + South Goa's only cardiac cathlab (Victor Hospital, Margao); named cardiology/neuro/ortho/GI/oncology programs, robotic surgery
notes: Real, specific specialist depth checked at Goa's own level, not assumed from India's national medical-tourism reputation

value_raw: [GAP]
source_ref: [GAP — no property.md exists for India; property purchase is a live HardConstraintRule bar for non-OCI/NRI foreigners, see IN-scope rows above]
notes: Buying isn't a live option for a foreign national with no Indian-origin ancestry — a real property.md would document the Overseas Citizen of India (OCI)/Non-Resident Indian (NRI)-only market instead of a general foreign-buyer one

value_raw: Short-term international/travel medical insurance, renewed per visa stay — available even to older travelers (some plans, no medical exam, up to age 85)
notes: The compound age-plus-visa insurability trap this criterion's definition warns about does not appear to bite as hard here as the definition's own 65-75-ceiling framing implies, for this specific tourist-visa-realistic route
₹12,000-25,000/month; Assagao specifically sits at the top of this band or above (gentrified into one of Goa's most expensive villages)

value_raw: Structurally constrained by the property-ownership bar — any group settlement would rest entirely on long-term rental, not freehold purchase
notes: A genuinely more precarious foundation for a multi-year group settlement than Guatemala's/Belize's/Mexico's freehold-purchase model — not independently tested against a real rental-market-only scenario this pass

fact_label: Subjective community read — party/backpacker-first, thin nomad layer
value_raw: A strong professional or nomad-specific community is described as lacking relative to this project's other candidates
notes: The dominant social texture is party/backpacker culture, not remote-work professional community; English less universally spoken than in India's larger cities

value_raw: Goa Mediclaim Scheme recognizes Mumbai hospitals (Tata Memorial — cancer, P.D. Hinduja) alongside in-state facilities
notes: A real, government-acknowledged ceiling on Goa's own tertiary depth for the most complex cases — read via a search-engine summary of the government PDF, not a direct verbatim read (a real sourcing-confidence caveat). Short (~1hr flight) medevac line by this project's standards, not a severe one
60% of cases specifically targeting women; a 20%-drop-in-peak-season-bookings tourism-industry finding named alongside it — real, current, quantified, not a vague reputation

value_raw: [GAP]
source_ref: [GAP — not evaluated as a distinct practice this pass]
notes: India's e-visa system is duration-capped rather than oriented around a repeated-short-re-entry culture — a read, not a confirmed enforcement finding

value_raw: Not a work-authorization category; up to 5yr multi-entry, 180-day cap per visit; 'routinely misused' for functional paid work
notes: The single most common source of visa violations for foreign nationals/companies in India, per multiple sources — does not lead anywhere structurally (renewable but not convertible)

value_raw: Illegal, incl. Goa, under India's Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act (NDPS Act); no government-authorized bhang shops confirmed operating in Goa (unlike UP/Rajasthan)
notes: Goa's counterculture/party reputation does not reflect its actual (strict, criminal) legal cannabis status — the same paper-vs-reputation gap this project's cannabis-research convention exists to catch

fact_label: Domestic private health-insurance maximum entry age
value_raw: No maximum age — a directive from India's insurance regulator (IRDAI) removed the ceiling entirely, effective April 1, 2024 (prior norm: new-policy cap ~65)
notes: Country-scoped, not Goa-specific — a genuine outlier relative to the 65-75 ceiling this criterion's own definition names as common elsewhere. Does NOT by itself confirm a non-resident foreign national is eligible to purchase this domestic product — see the companion [GAP] row below

value_raw: 30-day single-entry (non-extendable); OR 1-year/5-year multi-entry, capped at 180 days/calendar year per stay
notes: General figure across most Western/OECD-passport nationalities; full nationality-by-nationality breakdown not run this pass

value_raw: 1-year/5-year e-visas extendable via the Foreigners Regional Registration Office (FRRO)'s online e-FRRO portal in up to 6-month increments, max 1 year total per stint, USD 100-200 fee
notes: The 30-day e-visa cannot be extended at all

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Whether India's e-visa requires proof of health/travel insurance as an entry condition was not checked this pass

fact_label: Employment (E) Visa accepts passive income as qualifying proof
value_raw: No
notes: Employment/active-income only — no passive-income equivalent exists under this category
Tied to a single sponsoring employer; not applicable without a genuine qualifying Indian employer

value_raw: USD 3B net FDI in first 9 months FY2025-26 (described as an 'investment strike'), reversing 2024's 13% FDI growth
notes: India's overall investment rate flat-to-declining since 2014 (34.3% to 32.9% of GDP) — the "Modi paradox": political domination alongside a worsening economic slide

value_raw: No
notes: A foreign national of non-Indian origin, resident outside India, cannot purchase any immovable property in India at all, under any circumstances except inheritance — categorically stricter than every other candidate in this project's roster (Guatemala/Mexico/Colombia/Thailand/Morocco/Belize all permit some form of direct purchase). Not a soft continuum — a `HardConstraintRule`-shaped fact per the criteria-scale audit

fact_label: General Person of Indian Origin (PIO)/naturalization-adjacent PR route for a non-Indian-origin foreign national
value_raw: [GAP]
source_ref: [GAP — narrow statutory naturalization-by-residence provisions not directly researched this pass]
notes: No route found this pass by which a foreign national with no Indian-origin ancestry/spouse/long Employment-Business-visa history reaches PR-equivalent status in a realistic multi-year timeframe — a named open question, not a confirmed dead end

value_raw: BJP rules 21 states directly/in coalition, ~75% of population — strongest position since 2019
notes: 2024 general election itself required NDA coalition-partner support, constraining some policy execution

fact_label: No retirement, passive-income, or digital-nomad visa exists
value_raw: Yes (confirmed absent)
notes: Every real long-term route is gated on employment, business investment/management, marriage/family, or Indian-origin ancestry (OCI) — the single strictest visa-topology finding in this project's roster, confirmed across every source checked, not an oversight in the file

fact_label: Non-resident foreign-national eligibility for India's domestic health insurance
value_raw: [GAP]
notes: The more consequential open question than the age-cap finding itself — this project's actual reader (a non-resident foreign national, per the no-long-stay-visa finding) may not be able to buy the age-cap-free domestic product at all

fact_label: Overseas Citizen of India (OCI)-holder property restriction
value_raw: No (agricultural/plantation land specifically)
notes: Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) holders CAN buy residential/commercial property but are barred from agricultural/plantation land — a narrower restriction layered on top of the general non-OCI prohibition above

value_raw: Requires Indian-origin ancestry, OR marriage to an Indian citizen/Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) holder for 2+ continuous years
notes: Grants lifelong multiple-entry visa-free travel, exemption from Foreigners Regional Registration Office (FRRO) registration, Non-Resident Indian (NRI)-parity on economic/financial/educational matters; the Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) card itself went fully digital (branded e-OCI) starting May 1 2026. Not a route open to a foreign national with no Indian ancestry or marital connection, full stop

value_raw: ₹100-300/day (short overstays); ~USD 30 up to 15 days; longer risks 'Leave India' notices, fines, deportation, travel ban
notes: Exact thresholds between tiers not fully cross-checked — treat as directional

value_raw: Only 6 designated airports accept live-animal imports (Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata, Hyderabad, Bengaluru) — Goa's airport is NOT on the list
notes: A real, named logistical step for anyone relocating a pet to Goa specifically — not obvious from Goa's own tourism-facing information; whether an onward domestic-flight transfer carries separate rules not confirmed

fact_label: Pet import (dog and cat) — rabies titer / quarantine requirement
value_raw: No rabies titer required for entry; vaccination 30 days–12 months prior; 15-day quarantine only triggered by documentation failure
notes: A third genuine exception to this project's dog-only pet-import gap; up to 2 pets/passenger; no dog/cat divergence found beyond the shared rabies-timing rule
Managed, not fully free-floating — real firepower to manage rupee stability against external pressure
